title: Jaffna Crab Curry

ingredients.name: whole crab, cleaned and cut into pieces
visual_checks: Ask your fishmonger to clean and halve the crabs; break the claws so the sauce penetrates.
ingredients.notes: Ask your fishmonger to clean and halve the crabs; break the claws so the sauce penetrates.

subs.name: 800 g large prawns (shell-on) reduce braising time to 8 minutes — the sauce is equally superb.

ingredients.name: Sri Lankan roasted curry powder
visual_checks: Use the dark-roasted variety for Jaffna style; the quantity is high by design — this is a boldly spiced curry.
ingredients.notes: Use the dark-roasted variety for Jaffna style; the quantity is high by design — this is a boldly spiced curry.

ingredients.name: green chillies, slit
visual_checks: Reduce to 2 for a slightly less fierce curry.
ingredients.notes: Reduce to 2 for a slightly less fierce curry.

ingredients.name: tamarind paste
visual_checks: Dissolve in 3 tbsp warm water if using block tamarind; souring agent is essential to Jaffna style.
ingredients.notes: Dissolve in 3 tbsp warm water if using block tamarind; souring agent is essential to Jaffna style.

subs.name: 2 tbsp thick tomato pulp plus 1 tsp white vinegar replicates the sourness when goraka or tamarind is unavailable.
3 tbsp coconut milk powder dissolved in 200 ml water works as a pantry substitute.

ingredients.name: cooking oil
visual_checks: Coconut oil is most authentic for Jaffna cuisine.
ingredients.notes: Coconut oil is most authentic for Jaffna cuisine.

steps.text: Toss the crab pieces with 1 tbsp of the roasted curry powder, turmeric, and a generous pinch of salt. Mix to coat all surfaces. Set aside for 10 minutes while you prepare the aromatics.

Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high flame. Add the sliced onions and fry, stirring regularly, until deep golden — about 10 minutes. The dark caramelisation is what drives Jaffna curry's intense colour.

Add garlic, ginger, curry leaves, slit green chillies, broken dried red chillies, and lemongrass (if using). Stir for 2 minutes over medium heat until fragrant. Add the remaining 2 tbsp curry powder and stir for 1 minute until the masala is very dark and dry.
Add the tamarind liquid and stir it into the masala. Cook for 2 minutes until the masala comes together and the tamarind thickens slightly. The mixture will smell intensely sour and spiced.

Add the crab pieces and toss vigorously to coat every piece in the dark masala. Pour in the coconut milk and 100 ml water. Bring to a boil, then cover and cook on medium heat for 15 minutes, turning the crab pieces once, until the shells have turned bright orange-red and the sauce has thickened.

Uncover for the final 5 minutes to reduce the gravy and concentrate the flavours. Taste and adjust salt and tamarind. Remove the lemongrass stalk and serve hot with steamed rice — the sauce is best mopped up with fingers.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: crab → 800 g large prawns (shell-on) reduce braising time to 8 minutes — the sauce is equally superb.; tamarind → 2 tbsp thick tomato pulp plus 1 tsp white vinegar replicates the sourness when goraka or tamarind is unavailable.; coconut-milk → 3 tbsp coconut milk powder dissolved in 200 ml water works as a pantry substitute.
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Use live or freshwater crabs available at Indian fish markets. Cook in a thick-bottomed kadai rather than a pressure cooker — crab overcooks quickly. The dark-roasted curry powder can be approximated with a blend of 2 tbsp meat masala and 1 tsp dark-roasted coriander powder; increase red chilli powder by ½ tsp to get the Jaffna heat level. Tamarind paste is widely sold across India.
